blocks:
Ayahs

إِذَا ٱلسَّمَآءُ ٱنفَطَرَتْ (۱)
When the sky is rent apart, (1)
وَإِذَا ٱلْكَوَاكِبُ ٱنتَثَرَتْ (۲)
when the stars are scattered, (2)
وَإِذَا ٱلْبِحَارُ فُجِّرَتْ (۳)
when the seas are merged, (3)
وَإِذَا ٱلْقُبُورُ بُعْثِرَتْ (٤)
when the graves are overturned, (4)
عَلِمَتْ نَفْسٌ مَّا قَدَّمَتْ وَأَخَّرَتْ (٥)
then a soul shall know what it has sent ahead and left behind. (5)

Key Points

"Infiṭār" (انفطار) means splitting open. "Fiṭr" (فطر) and "Ifṭār" (افطار) also carry the same meaning, as the fast is broken (split) by eating food. "Infiṭār" and "Inshiqāq" (انشقاق) have the same meaning, but in Infiṭār, after the splitting, we reach something, unlike Inshiqāq. For example, when digging a qanāt (underground water channel), the Arabs say: "Faṭartu" (فطرتُ), because after splitting the earth, one reaches water. "Intithār" (اِنتثار) means scattering, and the things scattered over the bride are called nithār (نثار). "Fajr" (فَجر) means the emergence of a split and the opening of a path, and "Tafjīr" (تفجیر) indicates the magnitude and intensity of the splitting of mountains. The word "Bu‘thirat" (بُعثر) contains both the meaning of "Ba‘th" (بعث) (rising up) and the meaning of "Thawr" (ثور) (being overturned). Therefore, Rāghib stated in Al-Mufradāt that it is not unlikely that the verb "Bu‘thirat" is a compound of these two verbs, meaning the overturning of the earth and the rising up of the dead. The connection between the heavens, the earth, the sea, and the land is such that whenever the order of one of them is disrupted, the order of everything else is disrupted.

Messages

1- The order governing the heavens and the earth is in Allah's hand, and it changes whenever He wills. «اذا السماء انفطرت»

2- The Resurrection is physical, and the dead will rise from the graves. «اذا القبور بعثرت»

3- The disruption of the cosmic order is the prelude to the Resurrection, and the Resurrection is for the reckoning and recompense of human deeds. «علمت نفس ما قدّمت...»

4- On the Day of Judgment, human capacity expands, and the soul recalls all past deeds. «علمت نفس ما قدّمت»

5- Man must think both about his past, so that if he committed a wrong, he may repent, and about his life after death, so that he leaves behind a good legacy. «ما قدّمت و اخّرت»

6- On the Day of Judgment, man becomes aware both of what he has done and of the good or bad consequences of his deeds. «ما قدّمت و اخّرت»

7- On the Day of Judgment, man realizes the deeds he could have done but postponed and failed to execute. «علمت... ما اخّرت» And perhaps the intent of «اخّرت» (delayed) refers to deeds whose returns reach him after death, such as authoring a book or establishing corruption.

Explanations

Portrait of Sūrat Al-Infiṭār. This Sūrah has nineteen verses and was revealed in Mecca. Its name is derived from the first verse and means the splitting open of the heaven. Like other Meccan Sūrahs, the central theme of this Sūrah is the Day of Judgment and the conditions of people during that terrifying moment. The great events that occur at the end of the world's era and on the threshold of the Resurrection, the angels commissioned to record human deeds in this world, the ultimate fate of the righteous and the wicked, and a glimpse of the difficulties of that Day constitute the various sections of this Sūrah. Virtues have been reported for reciting this Sūrah in obligatory and supererogatory prayers, which indicate man's constant need for remembrance and attention so that he does not become heedless of the Day of Judgment and does not become attached to the world.

Ayahs

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلْإِنسَـٰنُ مَا غَرَّكَ بِرَبِّكَ ٱلْكَرِيمِ (٦)
O man! What has deceived you about your generous Lord, (6)
ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَكَ فَسَوَّىٰكَ فَعَدَلَكَ (۷)
who created you and proportioned you, and gave you an upright nature, (7)
فِىٓ أَىِّ صُورَةٍ مَّا شَآءَ رَكَّبَكَ (۸)
and composed you in any form that He wished? (8)
كَلَّا بَلْ تُكَذِّبُونَ بِٱلدِّينِ (۹)
No! Indeed, you deny the Retribution. (9)
وَإِنَّ عَلَيْكُمْ لَحَـٰفِظِينَ (۱٠)
Indeed, there are over you watchers, (10)
كِرَامًا كَـٰتِبِينَ (۱۱)
noble writers, (11)
يَعْلَمُونَ مَا تَفْعَلُونَ (۱۲)
who know whatever you do. (12)

Key Points

Gharra (غَرّ), derived from ghurūr (غرور), means heedlessness, deception, and being deceived (or arrogant). Sawwāka (سوّاك) means firmness, steadfastness, and distance from crookedness. ‘Adalaka (عدلك) indicates balance and equilibrium among the human limbs and organs. Rely on your own deeds, not on the generosity of Allah. The Prophets were aware of Allah's generosity, but they were people of action. Allah is Generous: رَبِّكَ الْكَرِيمِ (Your Lord, the Generous). His appointed angels are generous: كِرَامًا كَاتِبِينَ (Honorable Recorders). His messengers are generous: رَسُولٌ كَرِيمٌ (1) (A noble Messenger). His Book is generous: إِنَّهُ لَقُرْآنٌ كَرِيمٌ (2) (Indeed, it is a noble Qur’an). And His reward is generous: أَجْرٌ كَرِيمٌ (3) (A generous reward). Allah is Generous; He both forgives sins, conceals faults, and multiplies the reward many times over.

1) Hāqqah, 40. 2) Wāqi‘ah, 77. 3) Yā Sīn, 11.

Messages

1- Man is a heedless and deceived being who imagines himself safe from the consequences of his actions and the wrath of Allah. يَا أَيُّهَا الْإِنْسَانُ مَا غَرَّكَ بِرَبِّكَ

2- Why should man be arrogant despite all his need and weakness? يَا أَيُّهَا الْإِنْسَانُ مَا غَرَّكَ

3- The worst type of arrogance and pride is that which is directed against the Generous and Bestowing Lord. غَرَّكَ بِرَبِّكَ الْكَرِيمِ الَّذِي خَلَقَكَ

4- Man's arrogance and heedlessness are an abuse of Allah's kindness, generosity, and concealment (of faults). مَا غَرَّكَ بِرَبِّكَ الْكَرِيمِ

5- Allah's initial kindness and generosity are not exclusive to believers but extend to all human beings. يَا أَيُّهَا الْإِنْسَانُ.... بِرَبِّكَ الْكَرِيمِ

6- Man's arrogance indicates his heedlessness regarding divine blessings and the greatness of the Creator. مَا غَرَّكَ بِرَبِّكَ الْكَرِيمِ

7- The cure for arrogance is attention to His generous favors. الْكَرِيمِ... خَلَقَكَ فَسَوَّاكَ فَعَدَلَكَ

8- Creation is a sign of power, خَلَقَكَ (He created you); fashioning is a sign of wisdom, سَوَّاكَ (He fashioned you); and balance is proof of His knowledge of all needs. عَدَلَكَ (He balanced you).

9- The creation of man and the harmony and balance among his organs are manifestations of Allah's generosity. الْكَرِيمِ الَّذِي خَلَقَكَ فَسَوَّاكَ فَعَدَلَكَ

10- The diversity in the form and appearance of human beings is based on the wise Will of Allah. فِي أَيِّ صُورَةٍ مَا شَاءَ رَكَّبَكَ

11- To break arrogance, divine blessings must be pointed out consecutively. خَلَقَكَ, سَوَّاكَ, عَدَلَكَ, رَكَّبَكَ

12- He Who created you in this world will also create you in the Hereafter, so why the denial? كَلَّا بَلْ تُكَذِّبُونَ بِالدِّينِ

13- Whoever becomes arrogant due to this world denies the Hereafter. غَرَّكَ (deceived you) - تُكَذِّبُونَ (you deny).

14- Although you deny the Day of Judgment, the angels carry out their duty. تُكَذِّبُونَ... إِنَّ عَلَيْكُمْ لَحَافِظِينَ

15- Multiple angels are responsible for the preservation and protection of human beings. لَحَافِظِينَ

16- The angels are responsible for recording and documenting human deeds. كِرَامًا كَاتِبِينَ

17- The supervising angels adopt a generous approach when recording deeds. كِرَامًا كَاتِبِينَ

18- The angels are numerous, sharp-sighted, and meticulous. يَعْلَمُونَ مَا تَفْعَلُونَ

19- Attention to the recording of deeds by the infallible angels leads to modesty (Haya) and piety in man. يَعْلَمُونَ مَا تَفْعَلُونَ

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱلْأَبْرَارَ لَفِى نَعِيمٍ (۱۳)
Indeed the pious shall be amid bliss, (13)
وَإِنَّ ٱلْفُجَّارَ لَفِى جَحِيمٍ (۱٤)
and indeed the vicious shall be in hell, (14)
يَصْلَوْنَهَا يَوْمَ ٱلدِّينِ (۱٥)
entering it on the Day of Retribution, (15)
وَمَا هُمْ عَنْهَا بِغَآئِبِينَ (۱٦)
and they shall not be absent from it. (16)
وَمَآ أَدْرَىٰكَ مَا يَوْمُ ٱلدِّينِ (۱۷)
And what will show you what is the Day of Retribution? (17)
ثُمَّ مَآ أَدْرَىٰكَ مَا يَوْمُ ٱلدِّينِ (۱۸)
Again, what will show you what is the Day of Retribution? (18)
يَوْمَ لَا تَمْلِكُ نَفْسٌ لِّنَفْسٍ شَيْـًٔا ۖ وَٱلْأَمْرُ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِّلَّهِ (۱۹)
It is a day when no soul will be of any avail to another soul and all command that day will belong to Allah. (19)

Key Points

«Abrar» is the plural of «Bārr», meaning the righteous doer of good, and «Na'īm» means full of bounty. «Fujjar» is the plural of «Fājir», meaning splitting or tearing; that is, those who tear the veil (of modesty) and are insolent. «Salā» (صَلى) is entering into wrath and punishment. «Yaṣlawnahā Yawm ad-Dīn» (They shall enter it on the Day of Judgment) just as «Salw» (صَلْو) is entering into mercy and grace. «Huwa alladhī yuṣallī 'alaykum» (4) On the Day of Judgment (Qiyāmah), all affairs are in the hand of Allah, and this does not mean the negation of intercession (Shafā'ah), because intercession is also among the affairs that Allah has commanded. (5)

4) Al-Ahzab, 43. 5) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayān.

Messages

1- In education and propagation, compare the destiny of the good and the wicked. «Inna al-Abrar lafī Na'īm - Wa inna al-Fujjar lafī Jaḥīm»

2- Even the Prophet is not aware of many affairs of the Day of Judgment. «Wa mā adrāka mā Yawm ad-Dīn»

3- The matters of the Day of Judgment cannot be grasped by ordinary intellect; rather, we must accept them through revelation. «Mā adrāka mā Yawm ad-Dīn»

4- Important matters must be repeated. «Mā adrāka - Thumma mā adrāka»

5- The advent of the Day of Judgment is for the recompense and punishment of our worldly deeds. «Yawm ad-Dīn»

6- The criminals will remain in Hell forever. «Mā hum 'anhā bighā'ibīn»

7- The Day of Judgment is the day when ownership and authority are stripped from all human beings. «Yawma lā tamliku nafsun linafsin shay'ā»

8- The Day of Judgment is the manifestation of the absolute sovereignty of Allah. «Wa al-amru yawma'idhin lillāh»
